rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode — scan + value-score + ranked plan (`*surplus --plan`, Phase 1)
rows.summary: Build the read-only scan half of surplus mode: a workflow that fans out over .tad/active/ideas/, dream-candidates/, epics/ (parked phases), NEXT.md, and evidence/proposals/, plus an OBJECTIVES generator agent, then deduplicates, scores by value/token density, and writes a ranked SURPLUS-PLAN-{date}.md. Wire *surplus --plan in alex SKILL.
rows.cost_rationale: Creates 3 new files (workflow JS, template, surplus SKILL) and one-line modification to alex SKILL — well-scoped Phase 1.
rows.value_rationale: Directly enables systematic backlog monetization of surplus Claude usage, which is the core strategic objective of this Epic and directly addresses the user's stated goal.
rows.deliverable: .claude/workflows/surplus-scan.workflow.js created and passing, .tad/templates/surplus-plan-template.md created, .claude/skills/surplus/SKILL.md (scan path) created, SURPLUS-PLAN-{date}.md generated from a real backlog scan run

rows.title: Fix tad.sh:165 detect_state glob-arm hazard before next version bump
rows.summary: tad.sh 2.1*/2.2* arms (~305-313) will misclassify 3-part 2.19.x as v2.0 once TARGET_VERSION moves past 2.19.1. Must address before the next release bump.
rows.cost_rationale: Single file tad.sh regex arm update, cosmetic comment fix included.
rows.value_rationale: Release blocker — misclassification would cause incorrect upgrade behavior on all downstream projects.
rows.deliverable: tad.sh detect_state correctly identifies the current version after TARGET_VERSION is bumped past 2.19.1; no false v2.0 misclassification

rows.title: Merge epic/nondev-verdict-shapes branch after human review
rows.summary: EPIC nondev-verdict-shapes is complete (Gate 4 PASS) but branch epic/nondev-verdict-shapes is NOT merged — awaiting human merge decision.
rows.cost_rationale: Git merge operation plus smoke-test verification — no new code needed.
rows.value_rationale: Unblocks the non-dev delivery lane for voice, video, and product-thinking packs that are currently hard-blocked.
rows.deliverable: Branch epic/nondev-verdict-shapes merged to main; voice/video/product-thinking packs confirmed runnable in post-merge smoke test

rows.title: Structural gate reconciliation: align pack SKILL.md vs CAPABILITY.md byte identity
rows.summary: release-verify.sh structural flags 3 pack SKILL.md files (academic-research / ai-voice-production / video-creation) on every project because install.sh overwrites synced SKILL.md with CAPABILITY.md content. Must align or exclude pack-owned skill files before flipping TAD_RELEASE_GATE to hard-block.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches 3 pack CAPABILITY.md files plus release-verify.sh exclusion logic — 3-5 files total.
rows.value_rationale: Unblocks the hard-block release gate, making every future release structurally verified.
rows.deliverable: Either each pack's CAPABILITY.md equals SKILL.md byte-for-byte, or release-verify.sh excludes pack-owned skill files from byte-identity check; TAD_RELEASE_GATE can be set to hard-block without failures

rows.title: Surplus Burn Mode — budget-loop auto-execution + safety routing + dogfood (Phase 2)
rows.summary: Add surplus-execute.workflow.js: a budget-guarded loop that picks the top safe-to-YOLO candidate from the Phase 1 plan, runs it through yolo-epic.workflow.js (design→review→implement→impl-review→gate), tracks budget.spent(), and stops at budget ceiling. SAFETY-tagged candidates route to a 'needs you' list. Circuit breaker: max 2 retries → honest_partial → next. Final unified acceptance digest. Then dogfood on ≥1 real backlog task.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow file with budget loop, safety routing, and integration with yolo-epic.workflow.js — high complexity, YOLO gate discipline required.
rows.value_rationale: Delivers the full autonomous execution capability that turns surplus quota into durable reviewed deliverables, completing the Epic's core promise.
rows.deliverable: .claude/workflows/surplus-execute.workflow.js created with budget loop + safety routing verified, SURPLUS-REPORT-{date}.md produced from a dogfood run that completed ≥1 real backlog task end-to-end and archived it

rows.title: Triple-Question KA Evolution — Unified Knowledge+Skill+Workflow Reflection Loop
rows.summary: Extend Blake's Gate 3/4 Knowledge Assessment and Alex's *accept/*discuss exit points from 2 questions (knowledge? lesson?) to 3 (knowledge? reusable skill? reusable workflow?). Add workflow candidate format WCAND-{date}-{slug}.md alongside existing Skillify SCAND format. Wire Alex STEP 3.57/3.58 to detect both candidate types on session start.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches 2 SKILL.md SAFETY files (alex + blake) plus 1-2 template files; SAFETY zones require human review before merge, making this M not S.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1: directly surfaces upgrade directions from live execution (bottom-up capability accumulation), closing the gap between idea generation and implementation extraction — a research-grounded direction from the KR2/KR3 prioritization matrix (Epic B direction: agent self-improvement loop).
rows.deliverable: Updated .claude/skills/alex/SKILL.md and .claude/skills/blake/SKILL.md with triple-question KA blocks; new .tad/templates/workflow-candidate-template.md; SCAND→WCAND routing logic in Alex step 3.57; Gate 3 KA in Blake wired to emit WCAND when pattern type = 'orchestration'. Acceptance test: one complete KA interaction produces all three question outputs correctly routed.

rows.title: TAD Unified Auth Layer (`tad auth status`)
rows.summary: Create a `tad auth status` command that checks all TAD-dependent tools (gh, codex, gemini, notebooklm) in one pass with a unified output table, plus auto-runs at Alex/Blake startup to surface expiry before it blocks work.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope — shell script with 4 tool checks, session-start integration, and macOS-compatible timeout handling; well-scoped with clear evidence.
rows.value_rationale: Directly addresses a recurring real pain point (NotebookLM cookie expiry, broken auth checks, macOS timeout command absence) with concrete evidence from a recent session.
rows.deliverable: A .tad/hooks/lib/auth-check.sh script implementing `tad auth status` with unified table output for all 4 tools, integrated into session-start.sh to warn on expiry, with a fixed NotebookLM auth-check grep that handles the v0.3.4 table output format

rows.title: Goal-Driven Research — research-gate adoption + *sync to 14 projects (Phase 6)
rows.summary: Strengthen the *analyze Socratic/STEP 3.8 research-gate to actively suggest research when 'decision depends on external information' is detected (with a negative test for pure-config tasks that must NOT trigger), then *sync all Phase 4-6 research engine changes to 14 downstream projects.
rows.cost_rationale: alex SKILL modification is bounded; *sync to 14 projects uses existing sync machinery with post-flight verification.
rows.value_rationale: Propagates the already-validated research engine (Phases 4+5) to all 14 downstream projects and makes the trigger context-aware rather than opt-in.
rows.deliverable: .claude/skills/alex/SKILL.md STEP 3.8 research-gate contains explicit trigger-condition text + negative-test exclusion list; *sync post-flight report confirms research_plan_protocol + dormant hook present in all 14 registered projects

rows.title: O1/KR2 Differentiator Validation — Head-to-Head Task Comparison vs Cursor/OpenHands
rows.summary: Validate TAD's 3 claimed differentiators (Socratic quality gates, knowledge accumulation, two-agent terminal isolation) by running an identical real-world task (e.g., add a feature to a public repo) with TAD+Claude vs Cursor Agent vs OpenHands. Document methodology, record token costs, error counts, and knowledge reuse. Produce an evidence-backed validation report.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires running TAD on a real task (large context), writing 3 comparative run logs, and synthesizing a verdict doc — L because multi-session cross-system work, not just file edits.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR2: directly closes the 'identified but not validated' status — the 3 differentiators found in Round 1 are claims, not evidence; this produces the evidence-backed validation required to mark KR2 ✅ and informs whether to invest in differentiator-amplifying Epics.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/o1-kr2-differentiator-validation/: (1) task-spec.md (identical prompt used across systems), (2) tad-run-log.md, cursor-run-log.md, openHands-run-log.md (each with cost, time, error count, revision count), (3) differentiator-verdict.md with evidence-backed accept/reject/amend for each of the 3 claimed differentiators, updating OBJECTIVES.md KR2 status from 🔄 to ✅.

rows.title: Trajectory Evaluation Harness — SLM-as-Judge Gate Quality (Epic C Phase 1-2)
rows.summary: Build a lightweight eval harness that scores TAD agent reasoning quality per-step using a cheap SLM (Haiku) as judge. Input: existing *optimize trace JSONL files. Output: per-step scores on a 7-dimension CLEAR-derived rubric. Target: 0.80 Spearman correlation with human judgment. Directly replaces manual JSONL parsing in *optimize pipeline.
rows.cost_rationale: 2-4 files: rubric design + judge script + 3 baseline evals; no new abstraction layer, reuses existing trace JSONL format.
rows.value_rationale: O2/KR1: research ranks this Tier 1 with 98% cost reduction and 0.87 accuracy (SLM-as-Judge benchmark) — fills the 'Trajectory Evaluation Harness' Epic C outlined in KR2/KR3 findings, currently absent from backlog as a concrete execution item.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/evals/trajectory-eval-harness/ directory containing: (1) rubric.md with 7 scored dimensions calibrated to TAD, (2) judge.sh script invoking Haiku API on trace JSONL, (3) baseline scores for ≥3 existing trace files (menu-snap or equivalent), (4) Spearman correlation report vs human spot-check showing ≥0.75.

rows.title: Goal-Driven Research — Research-Decision Loop + --caller flag (Phase 3)
rows.summary: Add research→decision traceability to handoff §11 Decision Summary (Research Source column), decision→action→result feedback to OBJECTIVES.md Key Results, and --caller flag in research-notebook SKILL to enforce tool-layer capability fencing for Blake.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches alex SKILL, research-notebook SKILL, and handoff template — moderate cross-file scope.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the strategic loop between research findings and measured outcomes, making the research director genuinely director-level rather than execution-only.
rows.deliverable: alex/SKILL.md *accept step updated to check/update OBJECTIVES Key Results; research-notebook SKILL.md --caller flag enforced with forbidden-command list for blake; handoff template §11 includes Research Source column

rows.title: Run *evolve cross-project across 5 projects with 50+ trace files
rows.summary: 5 projects have accumulated traces (50+ total). Running *evolve cross-project will surface framework-level improvement candidates from real multi-project execution data.
rows.cost_rationale: Cross-project *evolve invocation requiring coordination across 5 project traces, no new code.
rows.value_rationale: First cross-project *evolve run with real v2 trace data — highest-signal input for TAD self-improvement.
rows.deliverable: *evolve cross-project run completes across all 5 projects; dream-scanner emits ≥1 framework-scope candidate with ≥2-project provenance; top candidate reviewed and actioned

rows.title: Complete EPIC: Agent Capability Packs remaining phases (Phase 2-4)
rows.summary: EPIC-20260507-agent-capability-packs has 3 phases remaining: Phase 2 (real project validation with menu-snap), Phase 3 (cross-agent validation on Codex), Phase 4 (template extraction for CONSUMES/PRODUCES standard).
rows.cost_rationale: Multi-phase Epic spanning real-project validation, cross-agent testing, and template design across 8 packs.
rows.value_rationale: Validates pack quality in real conditions and establishes the standard interface for all future capability packs.
rows.deliverable: Phase 2: quality delta measured on menu-snap with 2+ packs; Phase 3: same packs run on Codex with passing eval; Phase 4: CONSUMES/PRODUCES template extracted and applied to all 8 packs

rows.title: Local Skill Capture Mechanism (*save-skill)
rows.summary: Add a *save-skill command so users can capture reusable solutions from the current session into a project-local skill file, physically isolated from TAD framework skills so *sync never overwrites them.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope — needs directory isolation spike, *sync compatibility verification, and LLM-draft generation logic.
rows.value_rationale: Addresses a real gap: valuable session-specific solutions currently go uncaptured; in-context timing dramatically improves capture accuracy.
rows.deliverable: A *save-skill skill at .claude/skills/save-skill/SKILL.md that extracts current session context, generates a SKILL.md draft in .claude/skills/local/{slug}/SKILL.md, and verifies *sync exclude logic skips the local/ directory

rows.title: O1/KR3 Gap Severity Matrix — Rate All 5+ Identified Gaps by Production Impact
rows.summary: The O1/KR3 research identified ≥5 capability gaps (async execution, graph memory, checkpoint recovery, trajectory eval, proactive escalation) but their severity ratings are informal notes scattered across two findings files. Consolidate into a single severity matrix with a defined 3-tier rubric (P0/P1/P2 by: production failure frequency × workaround cost × implementation effort), then update OBJECTIVES.md KR3 status.
rows.cost_rationale: Single new synthesis file, inputs are two already-read findings files; no code changes, pure analysis — straightforward S scope.
rows.value_rationale: O1/KR3: gap identification is done but severity assessment (the 'with impact rating' part of the KR target) is missing — this turns an informal list into the structured severity matrix required to mark KR3 ✅ and directly informs O2/KR1 prioritization.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/o1-kr3-gap-severity-matrix.md containing: (1) 3-tier severity rubric with definitions, (2) all ≥5 gaps rated P0/P1/P2 with justification citing existing findings files, (3) 'top 2 P0 gaps TAD should address next' recommendation section. OBJECTIVES.md KR3 updated from 🔄 to ✅.

rows.title: Bundle dream-scanner Pass C dedup + scope hardening into one handoff
rows.summary: Three Pass C issues: (a) no dedup against existing project-knowledge before emit; (b) file=null causes override candidates to mis-classify as project even when framework-scoped; (c) line ~183 fromjson-error on malformed context leaks junk candidates.
rows.cost_rationale: Three coordinated fixes in dream-scanner.sh with fixture validation.
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates junk and duplicate dream candidates, improving signal quality for *evolve and *optimize.
rows.deliverable: Pass C deduplicates new candidates against existing project-knowledge entries; file=null override candidates correctly classify as framework-scope; malformed context in fromjson yields empty string not junk candidate; all 3 verified by unit fixture

rows.title: O3/KR3 Round 4 Deep Ask — Async Execution + MCP Ecosystem + Agent Eval Gaps
rows.summary: Conduct a 4th structured NotebookLM deep-ask round on the 3 topics OBJECTIVES.md explicitly calls out as still needed: (1) async/background execution patterns, (2) MCP ecosystem trends (tool sprawl, collision detection), (3) agent evaluation benchmarks beyond SWE-bench. Save findings as a dated .md file to advance O3/KR3 from 3 to 4+ deep-ask rounds.
rows.cost_rationale: Single output file, existing NotebookLM notebook (37cfefa5) already has 45 curated sources — execution is a focused query session, no new abstraction.
rows.value_rationale: O3/KR3: currently at 3/5 required deep-ask rounds; async execution + MCP ecosystem + eval gaps are the 3 topics OBJECTIVES.md names as still-needed research — this directly moves the needle toward ✅ and feeds O2/KR1 with new upgrade candidates.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/2026-06-{DD}-o3-kr3-round4-deep-ask-findings.md containing: ≥3 structured finding blocks (one per topic), each with a concrete TAD implication and cited source. OBJECTIVES.md comment updated to reflect 4 rounds and new findings file path.

rows.title: Fix multi-table §11 decision parser re-bind in emit_decision_points
rows.summary: emit_decision_points locks havehdr on the FIRST Decision Summary table and never re-binds, causing 2nd+ decision tables in one §11 to be silently dropped and a trailing non-Decision table (§11.3 disposition) to over-emit with stale indices.
rows.cost_rationale: Single hook/lib file change to the awk parser logic.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents silent data loss from trace emission when handoffs have multiple decision tables.
rows.deliverable: emit_decision_points correctly re-binds havehdr for each new decision table; a handoff with 2 Decision Summary tables emits both correctly; non-decision trailing tables do not over-emit

rows.title: Re-auth codex and run regen-codex-editions.sh to verify live happy path
rows.summary: regen-codex-editions.sh live happy-path is unproven due to codex auth token_revoked (401). Re-auth codex then run bash .tad/codex/regen-codex-editions.sh once and review git diff.
rows.cost_rationale: Auth fix + single script invocation + diff review — no code changes expected.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the one remaining environmental residual from the Codex-Edition Parity Epic, proving the release gate end-to-end.
rows.deliverable: regen-codex-editions.sh completes successfully (exit 0); codex editions regenerated and git diff reviewed and committed; parity-check.sh exits 0 post-regen

rows.title: Add durable express:true frontmatter marker consumed by layer2-audit
rows.summary: Current slug-naming convention false-WARNs any express handoff that forgets the name in the slug. Durable fix = express: true frontmatter field consumed by layer2-audit instead of slug-as-proxy.
rows.cost_rationale: Touches handoff frontmatter spec, layer2-audit parsing logic, and possibly alex/blake SKILL documentation.
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates recurring false warnings that erode trust in the audit tool's signal.
rows.deliverable: layer2-audit reads express: true frontmatter field; an express handoff without 'express' in the slug no longer triggers false ≥2-reviewer WARN

rows.title: IDEA: Automated TAD behavior validation self-test agent
rows.summary: IDEA-20260401: create an automated TAD behavior validation agent that can smoke-test key protocol behaviors (gate flows, handoff lifecycle, skill routing) without a human.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow/agent design requiring fixture creation, SKILL integration, and release runbook wiring.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces manual verification overhead on every release and catches protocol regressions before they reach downstream projects.
rows.deliverable: Self-test agent script or workflow that runs ≥5 TAD behavioral checks (gate routing, handoff detection, skill invocation) and exits 0/1; integrated into release runbook as optional smoke-test step

rows.title: Run *optimize on menu-snap (14 trace files) to analyze execution patterns
rows.summary: menu-snap has 14 trace files accumulated. Running *optimize will analyze execution patterns and surface improvement candidates for the TAD workflow.
rows.cost_rationale: Single invocation of an existing command against existing trace files — no code changes.
rows.value_rationale: Extracts learning from real project execution data, feeding the auto-evolve loop with concrete signal.
rows.deliverable: *optimize run produces a dream-scanner output with ≥1 actionable candidate from menu-snap trace data; results reviewed and top candidate filed as IDEA or handoff

rows.title: Dogfood Universal AC-driven Gate on Colin声音播客 non-dev handoff
rows.summary: Universal Gate (AC-driven verification) has no non-dev dogfood — the real non-dev handoff (Colin声音播客) end-to-end has not been run. Residual from COMPLETE item.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires running a full handoff on an external project — cross-project coordination and gate validation.
rows.value_rationale: Validates the universal gate works for non-dev tasks, which is the key untested scenario after completion.
rows.deliverable: A real non-dev handoff executed on Colin声音播客 project using the new AC-driven gate; Gate 3 + Gate 4 pass recorded; any behavioral differences from prior hardcoded gates documented in gate-design.md

rows.title: Unified Agent Adapter Pattern (TAD on Codex/Gemini/Cursor)
rows.summary: Build a protocol abstraction layer that auto-detects the running agent platform (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) and adapts TAD's multi-step task invocation to each platform's native API.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope — multi-round protocol is more complex than html-anything single-round, and parser fragility risk from upstream schema changes requires robust design.
rows.value_rationale: Directly supports the TAD Universal Method direction (memory entry project_tad-universal-method.md) and the Codex adapter validation already confirmed as a priority.
rows.deliverable: A .tad/agents/detect.sh script that scans PATH for supported agents and outputs a detected-platform.json, plus adapter stubs in .tad/agents/{codex,gemini}-adapter.md documenting the invocation protocol differences for the top 3 platforms

rows.title: IDEA: Unified agent detection + invocation protocol (TAD cross-agent infra)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: design a unified agent detection + invocation protocol so TAD workflows run consistently across Claude Code, Codex CLI, and other agents without per-agent branching.
rows.cost_rationale: New protocol design touching multiple SKILL files and potentially tad.sh detection logic.
rows.value_rationale: TAD universal method direction requires cross-agent portability — this is the foundational infrastructure piece.
rows.deliverable: Agent adapter protocol spec (DR) with detection heuristics for ≥3 agent types; at least one TAD workflow updated to use the protocol; passing Gate 2 expert review

rows.title: Capability Pack Codex YAML Adapter (openai.yaml auto-gen)
rows.summary: Add a --agent=codex flag to capability pack install.sh that auto-generates a minimal 6-line openai.yaml adapter from SKILL.md frontmatter, giving packs zero-cost Codex compatibility.
rows.cost_rationale: Small scope — 6-line YAML generation from existing frontmatter, with a clear reference implementation from ECC.
rows.value_rationale: Codex is already validated as a first-class platform (memory entry project_codex-adapter-validation.md); this automates remaining manual adapter work cheaply.
rows.deliverable: Updated capability pack install.sh with --agent=codex flag that generates .agents/skills/{pack-name}/agents/openai.yaml from SKILL.md frontmatter fields, verified working on 2 existing packs (code-security and web-frontend)

rows.title: ML Training Capability Pack — expert review + Colin project dogfood (Phase 3)
rows.summary: Run Layer 2 expert review (code-reviewer + backend-architect) on the built ml-training pack, fix any P0s, then dogfood the pack in the Colin voice project to guide at least one cloud GPU training session via Chrome MCP.
rows.cost_rationale: Expert review is bounded; dogfood depends on Colin project being in an active training phase.
rows.value_rationale: Completes the pack lifecycle and produces a real dogfood report validating the novel Human-AI Chrome MCP collaboration pattern.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/reviews/blake/ml-training-pack/ with ≥2 reviewers 0 unresolved P0 + .tad/evidence/research/ml-training-pack/dogfood-report.md documenting what worked/was-missing/was-wrong, pack registered in pack-registry.yaml

rows.title: Complete EPIC: Goal-Driven Research Director Phase 3 (Research-Decision Loop)
rows.summary: EPIC-20260504-goal-driven-research has Phase 3 remaining: Research-Decision Loop with decision traceability and --caller flag for the research director.
rows.cost_rationale: New workflow phase touching research SKILL, trace schema, and hook plumbing.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the research→decision feedback loop, enabling *evolve to use research outcomes as first-class trace data.
rows.deliverable: Research director emits a traceable decision record with --caller flag; decision traceability wired into trace v2 schema; passing Gate 3+4

rows.title: Self-Evolving Domain Pack — Trace-Driven Quality Criteria Optimization
rows.summary: Build three-stage auto-optimization: execution trace recording per domain pack step, failure pattern aggregation, and automatic quality_criteria update proposals with human gate approval.
rows.cost_rationale: Large Epic-level effort — needs trace infrastructure, aggregation agent, and safe approval workflow; open questions on trace format and aggregation threshold.
rows.value_rationale: Directly addresses validation theater from YOLO audit (principles.md entry) and closes the gap between structural verification and behavioral quality improvement.
rows.deliverable: A .tad/evidence/domain-traces/{domain}/{date}.jsonl schema spec + a trace-aggregator agent script at .tad/hooks/lib/trace-aggregator.sh that reads N traces and outputs a quality_criteria update proposal to .tad/active/proposals/PROP-{date}-{domain}.md

rows.title: TAD Universal Methodology Skeleton (Domain-Agnostic Framework)
rows.summary: Extract the Alex/Blake dual-agent + 4-Gate + knowledge accumulation backbone as a domain-agnostic skeleton, abstracting software-specific terminology so TAD can serve non-software domains.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope — requires audit of 6000+ line SKILL.md, abstraction decisions for deeply software-specific concepts, and risk of quality-chain weakening (v2.7 incident).
rows.value_rationale: Directly implements the TAD Universal Method strategic direction (memory project_tad-universal-method.md) and supports the user's goal of expanding TAD beyond software dev.
rows.deliverable: A new .claude/skills/tad-universal/SKILL.md that parameterizes the core TAD protocol (Socratic inquiry, expert review, gates, knowledge capture) with domain-neutral terminology, plus an audit of alex/SKILL.md identifying lines that are software-specific vs domain-agnostic

rows.title: Domain Pack Monthly Refresh — Tools & Skills Update Mechanism
rows.summary: Create a recurring handoff template or *maintain extension that checks domain pack tool versions, scans for new MCP servers/CLI tools, and produces an updated registry with changelog.
rows.cost_rationale: Small scope — a template file plus optional SessionStart hook timer; no structural changes needed.
rows.value_rationale: Domain pack staleness is a real problem given Claude's knowledge cutoff, and a template amortizes the recurring cost cheaply.
rows.deliverable: A handoff template file at .tad/templates/HANDOFF-domain-pack-refresh-template.md with step-by-step Blake instructions for monthly refresh, covering tools-registry.yaml version check and WebSearch scan for new tools

rows.title: IDEA: Capability pack Codex YAML adapter (6-line openai.yaml)
rows.summary: IDEA-20260527: add a minimal 6-line openai.yaml adapter so capability packs work natively in Codex CLI without the full SKILL.md install flow.
rows.cost_rationale: New adapter file format plus template documentation and at least one pack implementation.
rows.value_rationale: Extends pack ecosystem reach to Codex CLI users without duplicating the full SKILL.md install.
rows.deliverable: openai.yaml adapter spec defined and implemented for ≥1 pack; Codex CLI can invoke that pack via the adapter; adapter template documented in pack-build-rules.md

rows.title: Add --force no-op flag to academic-research and research-methodology install.sh
rows.summary: Two pack installers reject --force flag. Sync script patched a detect+fallback workaround (25/0 everywhere), but the proper fix is adding a --force no-op to those 2 install.sh files.
rows.cost_rationale: Two small shell script edits adding a flag passthrough — single-session, <30k tokens.
rows.value_rationale: Removes a known workaround in the sync script and makes installers consistent with the rest of the pack ecosystem.
rows.deliverable: academic-research/install.sh and research-methodology/install.sh accept --force without error; sync script no longer needs the detect+fallback workaround

rows.title: Add TAD:PROJECT-CONTENT-BELOW marker to 3 merge-strategy projects
rows.summary: 3 projects (my-openclaw-agents / toy / 内存管理) lack the <!-- TAD:PROJECT-CONTENT-BELOW --> marker so CLAUDE.md head is not synced. Need to add the marker or switch to overwrite strategy.
rows.cost_rationale: Single-line addition to 3 downstream project CLAUDE.md files.
rows.value_rationale: Ensures TAD framework updates propagate to all registered projects consistently.
rows.deliverable: All 3 projects have the marker in their CLAUDE.md and CLAUDE.md head is successfully synced on next *sync run

rows.title: SessionStart Full Component Health Check
rows.summary: Extend the SessionStart hook to validate TAD framework component integrity (hook scripts exist, config version consistent, template files not corrupted) at startup rather than discovering failures mid-task.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope — extends existing SessionStart hook with additional checks; list of components to check needs specification.
rows.value_rationale: Catching broken installations at startup prevents frustrating mid-task failures, directly relevant given the 'Never Hand-Write What an Existing Tool Does' principle incident.
rows.deliverable: An extended SessionStart hook script (.tad/hooks/session-start.sh additions) that checks hook script existence, config.yaml version match, and critical template file presence, outputting a structured health report with WARN/FAIL items

rows.title: Dream Scanner Auto-Scope Detection via Git Remote Hash
rows.summary: Automate *dream scope_tag classification by hashing git remote URLs as project IDs and auto-promoting candidates to 'framework' scope when the same pattern appears in 2+ registered projects.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope — modifies dream-scanner.sh and requires git remote access to all registered projects.
rows.value_rationale: Removes manual scope classification friction in *dream/*optimize loop, consistent with the existing ≥2-project promotion threshold already in TAD.
rows.deliverable: Updated dream-scanner.sh with a --auto-scope flag that reads sync-registry.yaml, hashes project git remotes, cross-references candidates across projects, and appends auto-detected scope_tag: framework to candidates meeting the 2+ threshold

rows.title: Security Domain Pack — ai-security + compliance YAML packs (Phase 2)
rows.summary: Build two additional security Domain Pack YAML files: ai-security (agent-runtime SSRF, OWASP LLM Top 10 via Garak/Promptfoo) and compliance (Fides CLI + SaaS-only mitigations). Includes tools-registry.yaml updates and cross-linking to existing code-security P4.8 safe_fetch architecture.
rows.cost_rationale: Two new YAML pack files plus tools-registry updates; compliance tooling is scarce so effort may exceed estimates.
rows.value_rationale: Completes the 5-pack security coverage and fills the ai-security gap not covered by existing packs, but blocked on real-project validation of Phase 1 first.
rows.deliverable: .tad/domains/ai-security.yaml and .tad/domains/compliance.yaml created with 5-7 capabilities each, tools-registry.yaml updated, cross-link to code-security P4.8 safe_fetch documented

rows.title: IDEA: Deprecate YAML domain pack format entirely
rows.summary: IDEA-20260508: deprecate YAML format entirely in favor of SKILL.md-based capability packs. 9 YAML packs (hw/mobile/supply-chain) still active.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires design decision, deprecation.yaml update, and at least one YAML-to-SKILL migration.
rows.value_rationale: Eliminates dual-format maintenance burden and aligns all packs to the proven SKILL.md architecture.
rows.deliverable: Decision record (DR) filed: go/no-go on full YAML deprecation with migration path defined; if go, deprecation.yaml updated and at least 1 YAML pack migrated to SKILL.md format as proof-of-concept

rows.title: Design semantic dedup for dream-scanner candidates (title/discovery match)
rows.summary: Current grep-on-.decision/.chosen dedup is inert (0/31 real matches). Needs title/discovery match or embedding-based semantic dedup to prevent duplicate dream candidates.
rows.cost_rationale: Non-trivial algorithm change to dream-scanner.sh requiring design + fixture validation.
rows.value_rationale: Current dedup is provably inert — duplicate candidates pollute *evolve queue and waste review cycles.
rows.deliverable: dream-scanner.sh dedup logic uses title+discovery fuzzy match (or embedding similarity); duplicate detection rate on synthetic test set ≥50%; 0 false-positive drops on distinct candidates

rows.title: Deprecate Domain Pack YAML Format
rows.summary: Sunset the 20 domain .yaml files in favor of Capability Packs (SKILL.md), since 12 remaining un-rebuilt packs are mostly unused and the research-methodology pack can regenerate them on demand.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope due to keywords.yaml hook routing changes and downstream sync to 14 projects, plus the open question of 4 related ideas becoming obsolete.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces maintenance burden and clarifies the pack architecture story, aligning with the memory entry showing capability packs supersede domain packs.
rows.deliverable: A deprecation plan document at .tad/active/proposals/PROP-domain-pack-deprecation.md specifying which of the 12 un-rebuilt packs to archive vs rebuild, updated keywords.yaml routing to reference capability pack triggers, and at least 2 domain packs archived with a git-tagged checkpoint

rows.title: Fix classify_scope word-boundary slug matching to avoid false framework classification
rows.summary: Unbounded substring globs *hook*/*trace*/*registry* false-classify project slugs as framework (e.g. webhook-handler→framework, registry-of-products→framework). Fix = bracket-class word-boundary per architecture.md 2026-04-24.
rows.cost_rationale: Small regex change in a single hook lib file.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents framework candidates from incorrectly fanning out across all projects via *evolve.
rows.deliverable: classify_scope correctly classifies webhook-handler and registry-of-products as project scope, not framework; existing framework slugs still route correctly

rows.title: Add collision signatures for GraphRAG and checkpoint/durable keyword overlaps
rows.summary: GraphRAG (rag-retrieval ↔ knowledge-graph) and checkpoint/durable (agent-memory ↔ agent-orchestration) keyword overlaps are not yet in collision-signatures.txt. Boundary note added but no formal signature.
rows.cost_rationale: Two new entries in collision-signatures.txt — single file, minimal change.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents silent rule contradictions between related packs that share domain vocabulary.
rows.deliverable: collision-signatures.txt updated with ≥2 new entries (GraphRAG boundary + checkpoint/durable boundary); scan-collisions.sh detects both when both packs are loaded

rows.title: TAD Self-Test Agent — Automated Agent Behavior Verification
rows.summary: Create a sub-agent that automatically verifies TAD components (hooks, skills, domain packs) are functional after upgrades or syncs, replacing slow manual terminal-based testing.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope — requires sub-agent spawning pattern validation, test harness design, and open questions around nested Skill tool invocation.
rows.value_rationale: Reduces regression testing friction after every TAD upgrade and sync, but TAD already has gate-based manual verification that partially covers this.
rows.deliverable: A .tad/tests/self-test.md skill file (or SKILL.md) that spawns a sub-agent, runs structural + behavioral checks on TAD components, and outputs a PASS/FAIL report to .tad/evidence/self-test/{date}-report.md

rows.title: Resume EPIC: Security Domain Pack Chain (Phases 2-4)
rows.summary: EPIC-20260403-security-domain-pack-chain is paused at Phase 2. Prerequisite: run a real-project security audit first to validate value before proceeding.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires real-project security audit as prerequisite plus multi-phase pack build work.
rows.value_rationale: Security packs address a genuine gap but value is unproven until the real audit validates the need.
rows.deliverable: Real-project security audit completed and results documented; go/no-go decision on phases 2-4 with evidence; if go, Phase 2 security pack delivered and passing Gate 3+4

rows.title: Audit and pin citation pointer URLs in 8 agent-adjacent pack findings.md files
rows.summary: Agent-adjacent pack findings.md reports have two numbering systems (top source-list vs report-body reference list) that may cause Source:[N] tags to point to wrong URLs. Claims are correct but pointers are ambiguous.
rows.cost_rationale: Audit and fix across 8 findings.md files — tedious but contained.
rows.value_rationale: Ensures research auditability per the YOLO audit findings principle — source URLs must be verifiable.
rows.deliverable: All 8 pack findings.md files have consistent [N] numbering with each Source:[N] tag verified against the correct URL; no ambiguous pointer remaining

rows.title: Security Domain Pack — security-monitoring pack + security-auditor integration (Phase 3)
rows.summary: Build security-monitoring capability pack and integrate all 5 security packs with the existing security-auditor subagent and Gate 3 flow.
rows.cost_rationale: Depends on Phase 2 landing first; integration with Gate 3 and security-auditor adds cross-file scope.
rows.value_rationale: Closes the monitoring gap and creates a unified security audit pipeline, but depends on Phase 2 completion.
rows.deliverable: .tad/domains/security-monitoring.yaml created with 5-7 capabilities, security-auditor SKILL updated to reference all 5 pack checklists, Gate 3 integration verified

rows.title: Cross-Project Skill Harvest and Promotion (*skill-harvest)
rows.summary: New *skill-harvest command that scans all registered projects' local skill directories, generates a cross-project index, and provides a review/promote/deprecate workflow to elevate local skills to TAD framework level.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope with hard dependency on local-skill-capture being shipped and used for 1-2 months first.
rows.value_rationale: Enables bottom-up skill accumulation across projects, but depends on local skill capture (IDEA-20260407-local-skill-capture) first accumulating data — currently no data exists.
rows.deliverable: A *skill-harvest skill at .claude/skills/skill-harvest/SKILL.md that scans sync-registry.yaml project list, reads each project's .claude/skills/local/, and outputs a HARVEST-{date}.md index with promote/copy/keep/deprecate options per skill

rows.title: Security Domain Pack — E2E validation on simulated + real project (Phase 4)
rows.summary: Run a full audit of a simulated vulnerable project and a real project using all 5 security domain packs to confirm end-to-end coverage.
rows.cost_rationale: Requires Phases 1-3 to all land first; validation itself is bounded in scope.
rows.value_rationale: Validates the full 5-pack chain but is purely a verification milestone with no new capability deliverable.
rows.deliverable: .tad/evidence/research/security-pack-e2e/audit-report.md with pass/fail per capability against simulated vulnerable project + one real project, 0 unresolved P0s

rows.title: GitAgent Protocol (GAP) Export for TAD Cross-Platform Portability
rows.summary: Map TAD's .tad/ directory structure to GAP format (agent.yaml, memory/, skills/, hooks/) so TAD skills and knowledge can run on Cursor, OpenClaw, Codex, and Gemini CLI without per-platform adaptation.
rows.cost_rationale: Large scope with high spec-instability risk; TAD already has Codex edition SKILLs as a more proven alternative path.
rows.value_rationale: Supports cross-platform portability but GAP is v0.1.0 and unstable — building against an immature spec creates maintenance risk.
rows.deliverable: A .tad/gap-export/ directory with a generated agent.yaml mapping TAD config.yaml, a GAP-compatible skills/ export of 3 capability packs, and a gap-export.sh script that produces the mapping automatically from the TAD source

rows.title: Hook Timeout Configuration
rows.summary: Add timeout_seconds configuration to TAD hook definitions so a hung hook script doesn't block indefinitely, with configurable behavior (warn/skip) on timeout.
rows.cost_rationale: Small if Claude Code already has timeout support; medium if TAD must implement its own wrapper.
rows.value_rationale: Prevents rare hung-hook scenario, but Claude Code may already have native timeout mechanisms — open question not yet investigated.
rows.deliverable: Updated .claude/settings.json hook definitions with timeout_seconds fields, and a wrapper in .tad/hooks/lib/ that enforces the timeout using shell timeout mechanics, with a test showing a slow hook gets cut off

rows.title: Domain Pack Taxonomy Reorg (cross-cutting vs vertical)
rows.summary: Add cross_cutting: true tags and a README classification index to domain packs, as a low-cost alternative to full rename/restructure of all 21 domain pack YAMLs.
rows.cost_rationale: Small scope using the lightweight approach (tags + README) rather than full restructure.
rows.value_rationale: Low-pain discoverability improvement, but demoted for lack of concrete user pain — the lightweight tag+README approach reduces risk significantly.
rows.deliverable: Updated .tad/domains/*.yaml files with cross_cutting: true frontmatter for cross-cutting packs (security, cost-observability, ai-tool-integration), plus a new .tad/domains/README.md with a vertical × cross-cutting classification table

rows.title: TAD Config Environment Variable Override Layer
rows.summary: Add environment variable override support to TAD config loading so CI/CD and container deployments can configure TAD_MODEL, TAD_CONFIG_PATH, etc. without editing YAML files.
rows.cost_rationale: Medium scope but uncertain value without a concrete CI/CD use case driving it.
rows.value_rationale: TAD is primarily a single-user local CLI; CI/CD use case is currently hypothetical with no documented pain point.
rows.deliverable: Updated config loading logic in .tad/hooks/lib/ (or relevant config script) that checks TAD_* env vars before reading YAML, plus a documented naming convention in .tad/docs/ or config.yaml comments

rows.title: DeerFlow Patterns: Persistent Memory Layer for TAD
rows.summary: Borrow DeerFlow's two-layer persistent memory pattern (session + user profile) to replace TAD's current manual project-knowledge file management with structured cross-session memory.
rows.cost_rationale: No concrete deliverable path defined — needs spike before implementation, and cross-platform concerns add complexity.
rows.value_rationale: TAD's 1M context + project-knowledge files already covers most memory needs; this is an enhancement without a clear pain point documented.
rows.deliverable: A design spec (.tad/active/ideas/deerflow-memory-design.md) documenting the schema for a session-memory + user-profile layer, with one implemented proof-of-concept entry in .tad/project-knowledge/ using the structured format
